intro: Stenoloma aculeatum, also known as the spiny wood fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the fern family (Aspleniaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Stenoloma aculeatum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stenoloma aculeatum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusions, and is commonly used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin disorders.
